notes: Evidence-only adapter: no Logic project file is parsed; the DAW is never observed live, written to, or asked to render.
Role inference is HEURISTIC with calibrated confidences: 99.3% over 6467 weighted filename instances (in-sample vocabulary coverage, not held-out generalization — see docs/evaluation.md).
Empty write/live_observation/render sections mean support NONE for every domain, not an unexamined gap.

properties.notes: Lead vocal chain: subtractive EQ into gentle compression, then de-essing (synthetic demo)
Kick and Snare Top are summed to a drum bus in the original session (synthetic demo)
Piano chain; Warmify Pro is a third-party plug-in outside the stock catalogue (synthetic demo)

properties.interpretation: The stem sum reconstructs the mixdown closely; little processing or content is unexplained by the exported stems.

properties.summary: Largest spectral difference: the mixdown has 13.4 dB proportionally less energy in 8000-16000Hz than the reference.

stem_sum_reconciliation.interpretation: The stem sum reconstructs the mixdown closely; little processing or content is unexplained by the exported stems.

hidden_state_markers.consequence: Temporal mix decisions such as vocal rides, send throws, or filter sweeps may be audible as a printed gain or timbre envelope, but the point-by-point curves and their targets are no longer editable DAW state.
hidden_state_markers.description: Automation state — Logic's track automation and region automation curves, their parameter targets, and per-track mode (Read/Touch/Latch/Write) — is not available from exported stems unless separately documented.

hidden_state_markers.consequence: The graph may represent exported stems as flat tracks even if the original Logic session used subgroup buses, track stacks, or VCA faders. The structures themselves are unrecoverable: a mixer group links controls without touching signal flow, while a VCA fader or a folder stack's stack master (which functions as a VCA master fader) imprints gain into each stem indistinguishably from individual fader moves.
hidden_state_markers.description: Routing state — sends (Pre Fader / Post Fader / Post Pan) to bus-fed aux channel strips, summing and folder track stacks, VCA group assignments, mixer groups, and sidechain sources — is not reliably recoverable from stem audio alone.

hidden_state_markers.consequence: An exported stem carries only the summed acoustic result of the insert chain: recommendations based on stem audio and filename evidence cannot distinguish printed processing from raw recording, nor recover plug-in identity, order, or settings.
hidden_state_markers.description: Native Logic channel-strip state — the serial insert-effect chain (up to 15 Audio FX slots, processed top-down in insertion order) and its parameter values — is not directly observable from exported audio alone.

recommendations.caveat: A vocal-like filename is not proof of a vocal recording, and printed processing cannot be recovered from stem audio alone.
recommendations.explanation: The graph contains a vocal-like stem, but there is no visible evidence of EQ, compression, de-essing, ambience sends, or other vocal-chain decisions.
recommendations.suggested_action: Add channel-strip notes or screenshots for the vocal chain if you want the session graph to represent editable production intent.
recommendations.title: Vocal processing state is under-documented.

recommendations.caveat: Level differences can be entirely intentional; this is a prompt to verify export settings, not a mixing verdict.
recommendations.explanation: One or more exported stems sit more than 6 dB above the session median, measured on silence-gated (active) RMS so sparse parts are compared fairly. This may be intentional, printed processing, or an export-level mismatch.
recommendations.suggested_action: Check whether stems were exported pre-fader, post-fader, normalized, or after bus processing.
recommendations.title: Potential stem-level imbalance detected.

recommendations.caveat: Descriptor patterns are suggestive only; they cannot confirm that any specific processing was applied.
recommendations.explanation: Some stems show descriptor patterns (low approximate dynamic range) that may reflect printed compression, saturation, EQ, or limiting, but the graph cannot identify the original Logic plug-ins or settings.
recommendations.suggested_action: Document whether stems were exported dry, post-insert, post-fader, or through buses.
recommendations.title: Printed processing may be present but undocumented.

recommendations.caveat: Name matching is heuristic and may miss valid pairings.
recommendations.explanation: MIDI or score evidence contains parts that do not clearly match an exported stem (e.g. Logic Full Evidence Demo). Linking them would improve the relationship between musical structure and acoustic outcome.
recommendations.suggested_action: Rename stems consistently or add a manifest mapping score/MIDI parts to exported audio files.
recommendations.title: Some musical parts are not linked to audio evidence.

recommendations.caveat: A reference is a point of comparison, not an objective target; genre and arrangement legitimately move spectral balance.
recommendations.explanation: Comparing per-band energy fractions (level-independent), the largest differences are 8000-16000Hz: -13.4 dB relative to the reference, 4000-8000Hz: -13.3 dB relative to the reference, 2000-4000Hz: -13.3 dB relative to the reference.
recommendations.suggested_action: Listen to the named bands against the reference and note whether the difference is intentional character or worth revisiting.
recommendations.title: Mixdown and reference differ noticeably in spectral balance.

provenance.explanation: Track reconstructed from exported audio evidence; no Logic project file was read.

warnings: Demo audio is synthetic (generated tones/noise), not a real Logic export.
Full-evidence demo: the mixdown is the exact scaled sum of the stems (gain 0.5), so a low stem-sum residual is expected by construction.
